title.en: Definitions & Acronyms: Why Every Word Is Load-Bearing
title.zh: 定义与缩略语：为什么每个词都至关重要

blocks.en: Clause 3 of IEEE Std 802.11-2024 is the dictionary you keep open beside everything else. It is not background reading you do once — it is a lookup table you consult constantly, every time a familiar-looking word appears in a sentence that imposes a requirement. The standard is written so that ordinary English words like 'station', 'associated', or 'frame' are *redefined* to mean one exact thing, and that exact meaning quietly overrides whatever the word means in everyday speech. Learn to reach for Clause 3 the instant you hit a term, and the rest of the document suddenly becomes readable.
blocks.zh: IEEE Std 802.11-2024 的第 3 章是你阅读时始终摊开在手边的“字典”。它不是只读一遍的背景材料，而是一张需要不断查阅的对照表——每当一个看似熟悉的词出现在带有强制要求的句子里，你都要回头查它。标准的写法决定了像 station（站点）、associated（已关联）、frame（帧）这类普通英文词都被“重新定义”为某个精确含义，而这个精确含义会悄悄盖过它在日常语言中的意思。养成一碰到术语就立刻翻第 3 章的习惯，整份文档会瞬间变得可读。

blocks.en: Clause 3 is layered, and the layers tell you how widely a definition is meant to travel. 3.1 holds general definitions — terms broad enough that the IEEE Standards Dictionary could also carry them (access control, channel, frame). 3.2 holds the definitions specific to IEEE Std 802.11 — the vocabulary that only makes sense inside this standard (the PPDU format zoo, the MLME/SME plumbing, RSNA, the mesh terms). 3.3 narrows further to terms that apply only when operating in certain regulatory domains, each tagged with a country code in square brackets. Then 3.4 lists every acronym and abbreviation, and 3.5 lists the regulatory-domain-specific ones. Knowing which layer a term lives in tells you how universal — or how jurisdiction-bound — its meaning is.
blocks.zh: 第 3 章是分层的，层级本身就告诉你一个定义的“适用范围”有多广。3.1 收录通用定义——这些词足够宽泛，连 IEEE 标准词典也可能收录（如 access control、channel、frame）。3.2 收录 IEEE Std 802.11 专有定义——只有在本标准内部才说得通的词汇（一大堆 PPDU 格式、MLME/SME 这套底层机制、RSNA、各种 mesh 术语）。3.3 进一步收窄到仅在特定监管区域运营时才适用的术语，每条后面用方括号标注国家代码。再往后，3.4 列出全部缩略语与简写，3.5 列出仅与监管区域相关的那些。知道一个术语住在哪一层，你就知道它的含义有多“普适”，或者有多“受辖区限制”。

blocks.en:
mindmap root((Clause 3
Definitions, acronyms,
abbreviations)) 3.1 General definitions broad terms, dictionary-shareable access control, channel, frame 3.2 Specific to IEEE 802.11 only meaningful here STA, AP, BSS, DS, RSNA the PPDU-format zoo 3.3 Regulatory-domain definitions tagged [US] / [EU] TVBD, white space device 3.4 Acronyms & abbreviations expand AA, AAD, MCS, NAV... 3.5 Regulatory acronyms tagged [US] / [EU] PLMR/CRS, TVBD, WSD
blocks.zh:
mindmap root((第3章
定义、缩略语
与简写)) 3.1 通用定义 宽泛术语，可与词典共享 access control、channel、frame 3.2 IEEE 802.11 专有 仅在本标准内有意义 STA、AP、BSS、DS、RSNA 一大堆 PPDU 格式 3.3 监管区域专有定义 标注 [US] / [EU] TVBD、白空间设备 3.4 缩略语与简写 展开 AA、AAD、MCS、NAV…… 3.5 监管区域缩略语 标注 [US] / [EU] PLMR/CRS、TVBD、WSD

blocks.en: Why does this precision matter so much? Because a single defined word can flip whether a 'shall' applies to you at all. Take 'station (STA)': Clause 3 defines it as a singly addressable instance of a MAC and PHY interface to the wireless medium — and then warns in a NOTE that this is *narrower* than the IEEE 802 meaning of 'station', which would include bridges. So a sentence that says a STA 'shall' do something is talking about MAC/PHY entities, not about every box on the network. Or take 'associated': association is a precisely defined service that maps a STA to an AP and unlocks the distribution system services. A requirement written for 'an associated STA' simply does not bind a STA that has only authenticated. Read the word loosely and you will think a rule applies — or doesn't — when the truth is the opposite.
blocks.zh: 为什么这种精确性如此重要？因为一个被定义的词就能决定某条 shall（强制要求）到底是否落在你头上。以 station（STA）为例：第 3 章把它定义为“对无线媒介的 MAC 与 PHY 接口的、可被单独寻址的一个实例”，并在 NOTE 中提醒：这个含义比 IEEE 802 中“station”的含义更窄——后者还包括网桥。所以当某句话说某个 STA shall 做某事时，它指的是 MAC/PHY 实体，而不是网络上的每一台设备。再看 associated（已关联）：association（关联）是一项被精确定义的服务，它在 STA 与 AP 之间建立映射、并解锁分布式系统服务。一条为“an associated STA（一个已关联的 STA）”写的要求，对一个仅完成认证、尚未关联的 STA 根本不构成约束。如果把词理解得含糊，你就会误以为某规则适用——或不适用——而真相恰恰相反。

blocks.en: Build one reflex: the moment you meet an unfamiliar term or acronym, jump to Clause 3 before reading on. For an acronym, go to 3.4 (or 3.5 if it carries a [US]/[EU] tag) and read the expansion; for a word in a requirement, go to 3.1/3.2 and read the full definition, including any NOTE. The definitions are alphabetical, and many cross-reference each other — an 'AP' definition leans on 'STA', which leans on 'MAC' and 'PHY'. Chase the chain until every word is pinned down. That five-second lookup is what keeps you from misreading a 'shall'.
blocks.zh: 养成一个条件反射：一碰到陌生术语或缩略语，先跳到第 3 章再往下读。遇到缩略语就去 3.4（若带 [US]/[EU] 标签则去 3.5）看它的全称；遇到出现在要求句里的词就去 3.1/3.2 读完整定义，连同其中的 NOTE。定义按字母排序，且彼此大量交叉引用——AP 的定义依赖 STA，STA 又依赖 MAC 与 PHY。顺着这条链一路追下去，直到每个词都被钉死。正是这五秒钟的查阅，让你不至于读错一条 shall。

blocks.svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 480 220' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1.5'><text x='20' y='28' fill='currentColor' stroke='none' font-size='13' font-weight='bold'>You hit a term in a 'shall' sentence</text><rect x='20' y='44' width='150' height='40' rx='6'/><text x='95' y='62' fill='currentColor' stroke='none' font-size='11' text-anchor='middle'>Is it an acronym</text><text x='95' y='76' fill='currentColor' stroke='none' font-size='11' text-anchor='middle'>or a word?</text><line x1='170' y1='64' x2='250' y2='44'/><line x1='170' y1='64' x2='250' y2='150'/><rect x='250' y='26' width='210' height='52' rx='6'/><text x='355' y='46' fill='currentColor' stroke='none' font-size='11' text-anchor='middle'>Acronym -> 3.4 (or 3.5 if</text><text x='355' y='62' fill='currentColor' stroke='none' font-size='11' text-anchor='middle'>[US]/[EU] tagged): read</text><text x='355' y='76' fill='currentColor' stroke='none' font-size='11' text-anchor='middle'>the expansion</text><rect x='250' y='126' width='210' height='66' rx='6'/><text x='355' y='148' fill='currentColor' stroke='none' font-size='11' text-anchor='middle'>Word -> 3.1 / 3.2 / 3.3:</text><text x='355' y='164' fill='currentColor' stroke='none' font-size='11' text-anchor='middle'>read full definition + NOTE,</text><text x='355' y='180' fill='currentColor' stroke='none' font-size='11' text-anchor='middle'>then chase referenced terms</text></svg>

caption.en: The Clause 3 lookup reflex: route every unknown term to the right sublayer of the dictionary before reading on.
caption.zh: 第 3 章查阅反射：在继续往下读之前，先把每个不认识的术语送到字典里对应的那一层。

blocks.en: One practical caution about acronyms: the standard reuses some letters, so always confirm the expansion in context rather than from memory. The most famous example lives in a NOTE in Clause 3 itself — a cryptographic 'message integrity code' is abbreviated MIC, *not* MAC, precisely because 'MAC' is already reserved to mean 'medium access control' everywhere else in this document. Expanding an acronym the lazy way is exactly how a careful reader trips. When in doubt, 3.4 is two pages of A-to-Z; finding the right line takes seconds.
blocks.zh: 关于缩略语有一个实用的提醒：标准会复用某些字母，所以一定要结合上下文确认全称，而不要凭记忆。最著名的例子就在第 3 章自己的一条 NOTE 里——密码学意义上的“消息完整性码”被缩写为 MIC 而*不是* MAC，正是因为在本文档其他地方“MAC”已被保留为“medium access control（媒体访问控制）”之意。偷懒地展开缩略语，恰恰是细心读者翻车的典型方式。拿不准时，3.4 就是两页从 A 到 Z 的列表，找到正确那一行只需几秒。

summary.en: What each sublayer of Clause 3 actually says — verified
summary.zh: 第 3 章每一层到底写了什么——经核对

blocks.en: The opening line of 3.1 instructs that 'the IEEE Standards Dictionary Online should be referenced for terms not defined in this clause' — a deliberate signal that 3.1 is the dictionary-shareable layer. 3.2 ('Definitions specific to IEEE Std 802.11') states its terms 'are specific to terms or references in this standard and are not appropriate for inclusion in the IEEE Standards Dictionary Online', which is why the dense format definitions and internal machinery live there. 3.3 and 3.5 both invoke ISO 3166-1's two-letter country designations, placed '[in square brackets] at the end of each definition / abbreviation that has clear attribution to a regulatory domain' (e.g. white space device [EU], television band device [US], PLMR/CRS [US]). The 'station (STA)' definition explicitly carries a NOTE that the 802.11 meaning differs from the IEEE 802 meaning, which 'includes bridges (or end stations)'; the message integrity code (MIC) definition carries a NOTE that 'the acronym MAC is already reserved for another meaning in this standard.' These NOTES are where the standard tells you, in its own words, that everyday usage does not survive contact with Clause 3.
blocks.zh: 3.1 的开篇就指示：“本章未定义的术语应参考 IEEE Standards Dictionary Online（在线标准词典）”——这是一个刻意的信号，表明 3.1 是可与词典共享的那一层。3.2（“IEEE Std 802.11 专有定义”）声明其术语“专用于本标准中的术语或引用，不适宜收入 IEEE Standards Dictionary Online”，这正是那些密集的格式定义与内部机制住在此处的原因。3.3 与 3.5 都援引 ISO 3166-1 的两字母国家代码，放在“每条与某监管区域有明确归属的定义/简写末尾的方括号内”（例如 white space device [EU]、television band device [US]、PLMR/CRS [US]）。“station（STA）”的定义明确带有一条 NOTE，指出 802.11 的含义不同于 IEEE 802 的含义，后者“包括网桥（或末端站点）”；message integrity code（MIC）的定义则带有一条 NOTE，说明“缩写 MAC 在本标准中已被保留为另一含义”。正是这些 NOTE，用标准自己的话告诉你：日常用法一旦接触第 3 章便不再成立。

cites.note: 3.1 General definitions header; instruction to reference the IEEE Standards Dictionary Online for terms not defined here
station (STA) definition + NOTE that 802.11 meaning differs from the broader IEEE 802 'station' (which includes bridges)
3.2 Definitions specific to IEEE Std 802.11 header; terms 'not appropriate for inclusion in the IEEE Standards Dictionary Online'
3.3 regulatory-domain definitions ([US]/[EU] tags via ISO 3166-1) and start of 3.4 Acronyms and abbreviations
3.5 Acronyms and abbreviations in some regulatory domains (PLMR/CRS [US], TVBD [US], WSD [EU])
